data.title: What's Behind The Jump In ENvue Medical Stock Today?
data.description:
• ENvue Medical stock is among today’s top performers. Why are FEED shares rallying?

Michigan Hospital Purchases ENvue Navigation Platform

ENvue Medical's catalyst is a fresh purchase of its ENvue Navigation Platform and feeding tubes by a teaching hospital and Level I Trauma Center in Southeast Michigan, expanding an existing relationship within that health system and taking the company's U.S. hospital footprint to 39.

The platform is FDA 510(k) cleared for adult use and provides real-time bedside visualization of tube movement, with management emphasizing "greater standardization" in feeding tube placement as hospitals prioritize safety and workflow integration.

That hospital-driven update is why FEED is acting like a momentum name today, with the stock up 55.26% at $2.22 at the time of publication. Traders are also keying off the company's push toward strategic account expansion nationwide, alongside longer-run platform expansion targets that include pediatric and vascular access applications.

ENvue Medical develops medical devices that help clinicians navigate feeding-tube placement more safely and efficiently. Its ENvue system uses smart feeding tubes with sensors, body mapping, and continuous visual guidance to confirm positioning and reduce complications like lung misplacement.

From a chart perspective, the move looks like a sharp rebound attempt after a weak stretch, with traders focusing on whether price can hold above key short-term trend gauges. The next question is whether this pop becomes a trend change—or just a fast mean-reversion bounce inside a longer downtrend.

FEED Stock Soars Above Key Averages

ENvue Medical is trading 25.3% above its 20-day SMA ($1.83) and 8.2% above its 50-day SMA ($2.12), which shows strong short-term thrust even though the 20-day SMA remains below the 50-day SMA (a bearish alignment that often signals the bigger trend hasn't fully healed). Shares are down 44.15% over the past 12 months, and the stock is positioned closer to its 52-week low (99 cents) than its 52-week high ($6.09).

Momentum gauges still aren't fully confirming a clean bullish turn: RSI is at 37.74, which is considered neutral territory but closer to the "weak" side of the range. Meanwhile, MACD is at -0.2859 versus a signal line of -0.2339, keeping the indicator bearish with a histogram at -0.0520.

The combination of RSI in the 30–50 range and bearish MACD suggests mixed momentum.

  • Key Resistance: $2.50
  • Key Support: $2.00

FEED Stock Soars Tuesday

FEED Stock Price Activity: ENvue Medical shares were up 51.05% at $2.16 at the time of publication on Tuesday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Image: Shutterstock

data.title: Ericsson's Vonage, ServiceNow Deepen Partnership To Transform Customer Support
data.description:
The integration allows calls to trigger automated workflows, incident categorization, and real-time updates within ServiceNow's CSM and ITSM platforms, reducing manual work and improving resolution speed.

Executives said the move enhances agent productivity and customer engagement, while improving generative AI performance through richer voice data context.

The companies will showcase the solution at upcoming industry events in Las Vegas in April and May 2026.

Technical Analysis

Ericsson is trading about 0.3% above its 20-day SMA and 11.2% above its 100-day SMA, keeping the longer-term trend pointed up even as the stock chops around near-term averages. Shares are up 39.11% over the past 12 months and are positioned closer to their 52-week highs than lows after setting a fresh 52-week high on 2026-03-17.

RSI is at 51.34, which sits in neutral territory and suggests momentum has cooled from the early-February overbought reading (RSI > 70 on 2026-02-06). MACD is at 0.1434 and remains below its signal line at 0.1940, with a -0.0506 histogram, pointing to bearish pressure in the short-term trend despite the broader uptrend.

RSI just above 50 alongside a bearish MACD indicates momentum leaning bearish, with bulls needing follow-through to prevent a deeper pullback.

  • Key Resistance: $11.50
  • Key Support: $11.00

Earnings & Analyst Outlook

Looking further out, the next major catalyst for the stock arrives with the April 17, 2026 (confirmed) earnings report.

  • EPS Estimate: 12 cents (Flat from 12 cents YoY)
  • Revenue Estimate: $5.72 Billion (Up from $5.15 Billion YoY)
  • Valuation: P/E of 12.4x (Indicates value opportunity relative to peers)

Analyst Consensus & Recent Actions: The stock carries a Buy Rating with an average price target of $9.30. Recent analyst moves include:

  • Morgan Stanley: Initiated with Equal-Weight (Target $11.00) (Feb. 9)

Benzinga Edge Rankings

Below is the Benzinga Edge scorecard for Ericsson, highlighting its strengths and weaknesses compared to the broader market:

The Verdict: Ericsson’s Benzinga Edge signal reveals a momentum-driven story with unusually strong support from growth, quality, and value factors. If the stock can hold the $11.00 area and reclaim the 20-day average, the setup favors continuation rather than a deeper trend break.

ETF Exposure

Significance: Because ERIC carries significant weight in these funds, any significant inflows or outflows for these ETFs will likely force automatic buying or selling of the stock.

ERIC Price Action: Ericsson shares were up 0.80% at $11.41 at the time of publication on Tuesday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

data.title: Vertiv Ramps Up Capacity To Feed Surging AI Data Center Frenzy
data.description:
Manufacturing Expansion In The Americas

The company will add or expand four facilities across South Carolina, Pennsylvania, and Mexico. These facilities will boost production of infrastructure solutions, power systems, and integrated cabinets.

The South Carolina sites are expected to increase regional capacity by approximately seven times. The Mexicali expansion is projected to lift capacity by about 45%.

Strategic Focus On AI Infrastructure

The expansion is aimed at accelerating deployment timelines and supporting scalable, high-density AI applications.

“Vertiv sees AI as a long-term, secular trend, and we are accelerating our capacity expansions to anticipate the continued growth in demand,” said CEO Giordano Albertazzi.

ThermoKey Acquisition

The transaction is expected to close in the second quarter of 2026, subject to approvals.

“Through our work with ThermoKey, we have come to value its differentiated heat-exchange technologies, engineering depth, and relationships across OEMs and system integrators. This acquisition is expected to expand the options available to our customers as they adopt more efficient cooling strategies and build infrastructure designed to stay ahead of rapidly evolving compute demands,” commented Albertazzi.

Technical Overview

Vertiv trades 1.6% below its 20-day SMA of $259.01. It remains 28.5% above its 100-day SMA of $198.36, maintaining a strong longer-term trend.

Shares have surged 173.24% over the past 12 months and trade closer to their highs. The stock is currently within a $53.60 to $276.78 52-week range.

RSI is at 55.10, in neutral territory, suggesting momentum has normalized after the mid-February overbought stretch. MACD is 10.3276 versus a 13.1067 signal line, a bearish configuration that often shows upside momentum is fading even if the broader uptrend remains intact.

With RSI above 50 and MACD still bearish, the setup points to near-term momentum leaning bearish, even as the bigger trend remains constructive.

  • Key Resistance: $255.50
  • Key Support: $234.50

Earnings & Analyst Outlook

Looking further out, the next major catalyst for the stock arrives with the April 22, 2026 (estimated) earnings report.

  • EPS Estimate: $1.00 (Up from 64 cents YoY)
  • Revenue Estimate: $2.65 Billion (Up from $2.04 Billion YoY)
  • Valuation: P/E of 75.1x (Indicates premium valuation relative to peers)

Analyst Consensus & Recent Actions: The stock carries a Buy Rating with an average price target of $199.11. Recent analyst moves include:

  • RBC Capital: Outperform (Raises Target to $266.00) (Feb. 12)
  • Citigroup: Buy (Raises Target to $286.00) (Feb. 12)
  • Goldman Sachs: Buy (Raises Target to $277.00) (Feb. 12)

Benzinga Edge Rankings

Below is the Benzinga Edge scorecard for Vertiv, highlighting its strengths and weaknesses compared to the broader market:

The Verdict: Vertiv’s Benzinga Edge signal shows a High-Flyer setup with strong Momentum and Quality. However, the stock has an extremely weak Value profile. Pullbacks toward support may occur quickly if growth expectations weaken.

ETF Exposure

Significance: Because VRT carries such a heavy weight in these funds, any significant inflows or outflows will likely trigger automatic buying or selling of the stock.

VRT Price Action: Vertiv Holdings shares were up 3.33% at $255.47 at the time of publication on Tuesday. The stock is approaching its 52-week high of $276.77, according to Benzinga Pro data.
